title: The Carpool Line Is My Roman Empire
subtitle: A field guide to school drop-off and emotional endurance.
summary: From forgotten water bottles to the one mom who always cuts the line—here is what parents vented about this week.
brandExplainer: The '90s Parent is a weekly letter on raising kids with humor, nostalgia, and just enough sarcasm.

entries.title: Why 7:42 AM Feels Like a Season Finale
entries.body: You packed lunches, found shoes, and negotiated a peace treaty over cereal. Then someone remembered it is spirit week.

entries.title: Three Phrases That End Playdates Early
entries.body: We are not naming names, but if you have heard 'watch this' near a trampoline, you know what comes next.

entries.title: What Your Kid Actually Heard
entries.body: You said 'five more minutes.' They heard 'forever.' A brief guide to translating under-10 listening skills.

entries.title: Snack Economics
entries.body: How did goldfish become currency? Readers report the going rate for one quiet hour of Zoom school.

entries.title: Poll: Worst Kids' TV Earworm?
entries.body: The results were traumatic. The write-in votes were worse.

entries.title: Next Week
entries.body: We tackle birthday parties, goodie bags, and the ethics of goodie bags that are mostly plastic.

disclaimer: Disclaimer: All names and identifiable details have been modified to protect the reputations of our contributors in the eyes of their partners, colleagues, and parents.
